title: poinsettia insulana
intro: Poinsettia insulana, also known as the Pincushion Mallow or Island Poinsettia, is a flowering plant species endemic to Hawaii. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
